title: Phase 1: Camoufox Runtime
overview: Add Camoufox (anti-detection Firefox fork) as a new browser runtime in browse_cli's pluggable engine system. This gives all 99 browse commands C++ fingerprint spoofing — navigator, canvas, WebGL, WebRTC, audio, fonts, screen, timezone, media devices, geolocation, speech voices, per-context identity, humanized cursor, and virtual display — by adding camoufox-js as a dependency and registering it alongside playwright, rebrowser, lightpanda, and chrome in the runtime registry. Phase 1 of 6 in the camoufox integration roadmap.
scopeChallenge: The runtime registry (src/engine/resolver.ts) already supports 4 pluggable runtimes via a lazy-loaded registry pattern. Camoufox fits this pattern exactly: camoufox-js provides launchOptions() that returns Playwright-compatible Firefox launch config. The BrowserRuntime.chromium field accepts any Playwright BrowserType including firefox. Two integration issues: (1) BrowserManager.launchPersistent() hardcodes chromium.launchPersistentContext() — profile mode won't work with camoufox without passing the runtime's BrowserType through, (2) CLI help text and error messages hardcode 'playwright|rebrowser|lightpanda' — need updating. Doctor command needs a camoufox check. Build needs --external camoufox-js. Tests need camoufox in AVAILABLE_RUNTIMES. CDP mode (--cdp) is incompatible with Firefox — needs graceful error.

prerequisites.assumption: Runtime registry pattern exists in src/engine/resolver.ts with BrowserRuntime interface
prerequisites.verification: src/engine/resolver.ts:20 — BrowserRuntime interface, line 58 — registry Record<string, RuntimeLoader>

prerequisites.assumption: camoufox-js npm package (v0.9.3) provides launchOptions() and Camoufox class
prerequisites.verification: npm view camoufox-js — published, exports launchOptions, Camoufox

prerequisites.assumption: Playwright ariaSnapshot() works on Firefox (used by browse snapshot)
prerequisites.verification: Playwright docs confirm ariaSnapshot is browser-agnostic; camofox-browser uses it on Firefox successfully

prerequisites.assumption: esbuild build pipeline supports --external flags for optional deps
prerequisites.verification: package.json:56 — build script already uses --external:playwright --external:playwright-core etc.

nonGoals: Per-context fingerprint rotation via browse commands (comes free with camoufox-js, no CLI exposure needed yet)

contracts.shape: BrowserRuntime { name: 'camoufox', chromium: firefox (BrowserType), browser?: Browser, close?: () => Promise<void> }
contracts.consistencyRule: If runtime.browser is set, server uses it directly; otherwise calls runtime.chromium.launch(launchOptions). Camoufox returns pre-connected browser via firefox.launch(opts).
CLI validates runtime name exists in AVAILABLE_RUNTIMES before spawning. Error message lists all available runtimes.

contracts.shape: Text line: 'Camoufox: installed (v0.9.3)' or 'Camoufox: NOT INSTALLED — run npm install camoufox-js'
contracts.consistencyRule: Doctor checks camoufox-js importability and binary availability via camoufox-js fetch status. Does not require camoufox to be the active runtime.

failureModes.risk: camoufox-js postinstall downloads ~300MB Camoufox binary — may fail in CI or low-disk environments
failureModes.mitigation: Install as optionalDependency. Runtime loader catches import failure and throws clear install instructions (same pattern as rebrowser).

failureModes.risk: Firefox ariaSnapshot format differs from Chromium — snapshot refs may break
failureModes.mitigation: Integration test runs goto + snapshot + click on camoufox runtime. If format differs, snapshot.ts parser already handles YAML-like format agnostically.

failureModes.risk: User tries --cdp with camoufox — Firefox doesn't support CDP natively
failureModes.mitigation: Server.ts checks runtime name before CDP connection. Clear error: 'Camoufox (Firefox) does not support CDP. Remove --cdp flag or use --runtime playwright.'

failureModes.risk: Profile mode calls chromium.launchPersistentContext — fails with camoufox runtime
failureModes.mitigation: BrowserManager.launchPersistent() currently hardcodes chromium import. Needs to accept a BrowserType parameter or read from runtime. Covered in TASK-003.

failureModes.risk: Camoufox binary not available on all platforms (e.g. aarch64 Linux)
failureModes.mitigation: Doctor command reports binary availability. Runtime loader error message includes platform-specific install guidance.

testCoverageMap.codepath: camoufox-js not installed → clear error with install instructions
Full browsing flow: goto → snapshot → click → text on camoufox runtime

tasks.title: Add camoufox-js dependency and build config
tasks.description:
Install camoufox-js as an optionalDependency in package.json (same category as rebrowser-playwright — not required for default operation). Add --external:camoufox-js to the esbuild build script so the optional dep is not bundled into dist/browse.cjs. This mirrors how playwright and playwright-core are handled.

Files:
- `package.json` — add camoufox-js to optionalDependencies, add --external:camoufox-js to build script

The optionalDependency pattern means npm install won't fail if camoufox-js can't download its binary (e.g. unsupported platform). The runtime loader (TASK-002) catches the import failure gracefully.
acceptanceCriteria: Build script (npm run build) includes --external:camoufox-js and completes without error
npm install succeeds even if camoufox-js binary download fails (optionalDependency graceful failure)
tasks.validateCommand: npm run build && node -e "const pkg = require('./package.json'); if (!pkg.optionalDependencies?.['camoufox-js']) throw 'missing'"

tasks.title: Register camoufox runtime in engine resolver
tasks.description:
Add a 'camoufox' entry to the registry in src/engine/resolver.ts. The loader:
1. Dynamically imports camoufox-js (catches ImportError → throws 'camoufox-js not installed. Run: npm install camoufox-js')
2. Calls launchOptions({ headless: true, humanize: true, enable_cache: true }) to get Playwright-compatible Firefox launch config
3. Dynamically imports playwright-core's firefox BrowserType
4. Returns { name: 'camoufox', chromium: firefox } — note: 'chromium' field is misnamed but accepts any BrowserType
5. Does NOT pre-launch the browser (server.ts handles launch via runtime.chromium.launch(opts))

Also add a findCamoufox() helper (exported, like findLightpanda()) that checks if camoufox-js is importable and its binary is available.

Update the barrel export in src/engine/index.ts to export findCamoufox.

Pattern to follow: the rebrowser loader (line 64-72) for import-failure handling; the lightpanda loader for process-runtime complexity reference (camoufox is simpler — it's a library runtime like playwright).

Key difference from lightpanda: camoufox is a library runtime, not a process runtime. It returns a BrowserType (firefox), and server.ts calls .launch() on it. The launchOptions() from camoufox-js provides the args/env needed for anti-detection.

The server.ts launch path (line 525-538) calls runtime.chromium.launch(launchOptions). For camoufox, the runtime must merge server's launchOptions (headless, proxy) with camoufox-js's launchOptions (args, env). Store the camoufox launch options on the runtime object so server.ts can merge them.
acceptanceCriteria: getRuntime('camoufox') returns BrowserRuntime with name='camoufox' and a valid BrowserType
When camoufox-js is not installed, getRuntime('camoufox') throws error containing 'camoufox-js not installed' and install command
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert resolver.ts and index.ts changes only

tasks.title: Wire camoufox runtime through server and CLI
tasks.description:
Update the server, CLI, target factory, and BrowserManager to handle the camoufox runtime correctly:

1. **server.ts (line 525-538)**: When runtime.name is 'camoufox', merge camoufox-js launchOptions with server's launchOptions (headless flag, proxy). The camoufox runtime stores its options object; server.ts spreads them into the launch call. Specifically: merge args arrays, merge env objects, preserve camoufox's firefoxUserPrefs.

2. **server.ts CDP guard (line 512-516)**: Before connecting via CDP, check if runtime.name is 'camoufox'. If so, throw: 'Camoufox (Firefox) does not support Chrome DevTools Protocol. Remove --cdp or use --runtime playwright.'

3. **server.ts profile mode (line 474-499)**: Currently calls `createPersistentBrowserTarget(profileDir, onCrash)`. Update to pass the runtime's BrowserType: `createPersistentBrowserTarget(profileDir, onCrash, runtime.chromium)`.

4. **src/session/target-factory.ts (line 50-66)**: `createPersistentBrowserTarget()` currently only accepts `(profileDir, onCrash)` and calls `bm.launchPersistent(profileDir, onCrash)`. Add an optional third parameter `browserType?: BrowserType` and pass it through to `bm.launchPersistent(profileDir, onCrash, browserType)`. This is the critical path — without this change, the runtime's BrowserType never reaches the persistent launch.

5. **BrowserManager.launchPersistent (line 239-265)**: This method hardcodes `chromium.launchPersistentContext()`. Add an optional `browserType` parameter. When provided, use it instead of hardcoded `chromium`. Default to `chromium` import for backward compatibility.

6. **cli.ts (line 676)**: Update the error message for --runtime to include camoufox in the list.
acceptanceCriteria: BROWSE_RUNTIME=camoufox browse goto https://example.com works end-to-end (server launches Firefox via camoufox-js)
BROWSE_RUNTIME=camoufox with --cdp flag produces clear error about Firefox CDP incompatibility

Profile mode (--profile test) with BROWSE_RUNTIME=camoufox uses firefox.launchPersistentContext — verified by checking that createPersistentBrowserTarget receives and threads the runtime BrowserType through to BrowserManager.launchPersistent
tasks.rollbackPlan: Revert server.ts, cli.ts, manager.ts changes only

tasks.title: Add camoufox to doctor command diagnostics
tasks.description:
Extend the doctor command in src/commands/meta/system.ts (line 131+) to check camoufox availability. Add a section after the Playwright/Chromium checks:

1. Try dynamic import of camoufox-js
2. If importable, report version and binary status (camoufox-js provides a way to check if the binary is downloaded)
3. If not importable, report 'Camoufox: NOT INSTALLED — run npm install camoufox-js (optional — for anti-detection browsing)'
4. Mark it clearly as optional (unlike Playwright which is required)

Follow the existing pattern: try/catch import, report status line. The doctor command runs server-side (it's a meta command dispatched through the server), so dynamic import works.
acceptanceCriteria: browse doctor output includes a 'Camoufox:' line showing installed/not-installed status
When camoufox-js is installed, doctor shows version number
When camoufox-js is NOT installed, doctor shows install command and marks it as optional

tasks.title: Add camoufox runtime tests
tasks.description:
Extend the test suite in test/features.test.ts to cover the camoufox runtime:

1. **Unit tests** (in the existing 'Runtime resolver' describe block, line 1215+):
   - AVAILABLE_RUNTIMES includes 'camoufox'
   - findCamoufox() returns null or valid status
   - getRuntime('camoufox') either succeeds (if installed) or throws with 'camoufox-js not installed'

2. **Integration test** (new describe block 'Camoufox runtime integration'):
   - Skip if camoufox-js not installed (use test.skipIf pattern)
   - Launch camoufox runtime, navigate to test server, verify page loads
   - Run snapshot command, verify @refs are generated
   - Run click on a ref, verify page state changes
   - Run text command, verify visible text extraction
   - Close runtime cleanly

3. **CLI flag test** (extend '--runtime CLI flag' describe block, line 1249+):
   - AVAILABLE_RUNTIMES includes 'camoufox'

Follow existing test patterns: use the shared test server (test/test-server.ts), BrowserManager for integration tests. For the integration test, use the runtime system (getRuntime + launch) instead of BrowserManager.launch() since that hardcodes chromium.
acceptanceCriteria: All new tests pass when camoufox-js IS installed: npm test shows camoufox tests passing
All new tests are skipped gracefully when camoufox-js is NOT installed (no failures, just skips)
Integration test covers goto → snapshot → click → text flow on camoufox runtime against real test server
